specific_mechanism: RIP-Router: certify Restricted Isometry Property RIP_s(δ) for the MoE router projection W_router. Estimate δ via random s-column-sub-matrix spectral norms; add RIP-aware regularizer R = (max_S ‖A_S^T A_S - I‖_F)² to training loss. Top-s expert selection then has L₁-recovery guarantee with bounded error.
llm_application: RIP-Router: at each training step, for the router projection matrix W ∈ R^{E×d}, sample K random s-column sub-matrices A_S; compute σ_max(A_S^T A_S - I), σ_min(A_S^T A_S - I). δ_s ≈ max(|σ_max|, |σ_min|). Add penalty λ·max(0, δ_s - δ_target) where δ_target = √2-1 - margin. This biases the router to satisfy RIP_s; stability of top-s expert selection follows by compressed-sensing theory.
novelty_claim: CSAT (arXiv 2507.02957) and Routing Transformers (arXiv 2003.05997) use compressed-sensing ideas for attention; CSAT is closest but for attention, not MoE router. RIP-Router targets MoE router specifically.
motivation_justification: RIP is the algebraic property guaranteeing exact L_1 recovery in compressed sensing. Direct transfer to s-sparse top-s expert selection.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Random s-column sub-matrix sampling of W_router
